title: elymus spicatus subsp. inermis
intro: Elymus spicatus subsp. inermis, also known as the spike false brome or inermis wild rye, is a plant species belonging to the family Poaceae. This perennial grass is native to certain regions in Europe and Asia, and its medicinal properties include its use in treating various health issues due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits associated with Elymus spicatus subsp. inermis include its ability to aid in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. In traditional herbal medicine, preparations of the plant are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties.
